nodes.name: Bertram's weaver
nodes.summary: Bertram's weaver (Ploceus bertrandi), sometimes called Bertrand's weaver, is a species of bird in the family Ploceidae. It is native to the Eastern Arc forests and mountains of Malawi. It is named after Bertram Lutley Sclater, the son of ornithologist Philip Lutley Sclater, who was a police commissioner in Malawi when Alexander Whyte discovered this species. References External links Media related to Ploceus bertrandi at Wikimedia Commons Bertram's weaver - Species text in Weaver Watch.
